13.summary: Senior specialist/individual contributor. Executes complex systems analysis, testing, integration, and development with some planning and architecture support.

duty_sections.title: Systems Analysis and Integration
duty_sections.description: Analyze business and technical requirements; plan and integrate system components across environments; ensure secure configurations and interoperability.
example_tasks: Analyze and translate stakeholder requirements into technical designs and integration plans.
Define secure configuration baselines and ensure RMF-aligned controls are applied.
Coordinate with owners to integrate components and data flows across test/pre-prod/prod.

duty_sections.title: Test and Evaluation
duty_sections.description: Plan and execute test strategies; operate and manage test environments; measure results and recommend fixes; support release readiness.
example_tasks: Develop test plans, cases, and data; validate requirements for testability.
Operate and patch test environments in sync with production; manage configuration.
Analyze/report test results; recommend acceptance, remediation, or design changes.

duty_sections.title: Programming / Applications Development
duty_sections.description: Design, develop, and maintain applications or scripts; create documentation; support integration with enterprise systems.
example_tasks: Design and develop application code; create workflow charts and technical specs.
Implement unit/integration tests; validate and document results.
Refactor, optimize, and maintain code; integrate COTS/APIs and shared services.

duty_sections.title: Enterprise/Data Architecture Support
duty_sections.description: Support enterprise/data architecture models and metadata; perform data validation and quality checks; document current/target states.
example_tasks: Develop and maintain data models, metadata, and lineage for migration efforts.
Document architecture views and interface inventories using standard notations.
Perform data profiling, quality checks, and remediation planning.

14.summary: Senior IT specialist/project manager. Leads cross-functional projects and integrations; balances PM, systems analysis, software/DevSecOps, and governance/authorization.

duty_sections.title: Project/Program Management
duty_sections.description: Define scope, schedule, and budget; lead risk, acquisition, and change control; coordinate delivery across organizations.
example_tasks: Develop and manage integrated master schedules, budgets, risks, and metrics.
Lead acquisition planning, evaluations, and COR oversight; ensure compliance.
Execute Agile/iterative delivery; manage releases and change control.

duty_sections.title: Systems Analysis and Integration
duty_sections.description: Develop technical plans and architectures; conduct cost/benefit and alternatives analyses; coordinate enterprise integration.
example_tasks: Author technical plans (architecture, interfaces, hosting, security, contingency).
Coordinate multi-agency integration and data interoperability.
Perform trade studies and business case analyses to inform decisions.

duty_sections.title: Applications Software / DevSecOps
duty_sections.description: Lead software design, implementation, and testing; integrate COTS with legacy; embed security and quality throughout the lifecycle.
example_tasks: Oversee design/development; enforce secure coding and test automation.
Integrate COTS/SaaS with enterprise services and data; ensure performance and resilience.
Plan and lead migration, modernization, and decommissioning of legacy apps.

duty_sections.title: Governance, Authorization, and Communications
duty_sections.description: Lead governance bodies and ATO activities; brief senior leaders; manage stakeholder communications and decisions.
example_tasks: Prepare and review authorization packages; define acceptable risk thresholds.
Lead IPTs/working groups; present status, risks, and mitigation plans to leadership.
Establish standards and policies; ensure compliance and traceability.

15.summary: Supervisory IT Program Manager. Provides portfolio-level strategy, directs execution of multiple programs/projects, and leads people and resources.

duty_sections.title: Strategic Planning and Acquisition Governance
duty_sections.description: Set program vision, strategies, and roadmaps; lead acquisition strategies and budget formulation; align architecture and policy.
example_tasks: Establish multi-year roadmaps, goals, and KPIs aligned to agency priorities.
Lead acquisition planning and source selections; shape contracts to outcomes.
Align enterprise architecture, security, and policy with mission objectives.

duty_sections.title: Program Execution and Oversight
duty_sections.description: Direct execution across multiple projects; monitor performance, quality, risk, and compliance; remove barriers and resolve issues.
example_tasks: Chair program reviews; track schedule, cost, scope, and benefits realization.
Resolve cross-program issues; negotiate priorities with internal/external partners.
Establish internal controls; ensure audits, security, and policy adherence.

duty_sections.title: Supervision and Workforce Management
duty_sections.description: Lead teams and supervisors; set priorities and standards; manage hiring, performance, development, and labor/EEO relations.
example_tasks: Assign and evaluate work; set performance plans; recognize and address issues.
Hire, develop, and mentor staff; plan training and succession; manage remote teams.
Foster inclusive culture; maintain EEO compliance and effective labor relations.

notes.weighting_guidance: Select weights within the provided ranges so sections sum to 100%. Emphasize leadership/strategy more at higher grades and technical execution more at GS-13.
notes.authoritative_basis: Synthesized from VA GS-2210 PDs in inputs/VA_Requests/Processed (GS-13, GS-14, GS-15 examples).
notes.usage: Choose a GS level to generate duty sections with weights and tasks; customize domain terms (e.g., EHRM) and technologies to your program context.
